answers.title: 失而复得的尼康
answers.content:
Speaking of which, I want to share this story with you.

I have been fond of(喜欢) photography for a long time, and my dream is to become a professional photographer in the future. I am very lucky because my parents are very supportive of(支持…) my choice. On my fourteenth birthday, my father gave me a brand new state-of-the-art Nikon camera at my birthday party. My parents said they wanted nothing in return apart from my happiness(父母说他们只希望我幸福). As soon as I got the camera, I was so excited that I almost cried. I was overjoyed because I finally had my own camera and I was one step closer to my dream(我离自己的梦想更近了一步). I assured my parents that I would make good use of this camera to document our lives(记录我们的生活).

But luck was not on my side(不过幸运之神并不眷顾于我). A year later, my parents and I went to Huangshan for a walking holiday. On one of the days it was raining heavily and the road was slippery. I had hung the camera around my neck for fear of accidentally breaking it(生怕不小心把相机磕坏了). But while climbing up a steep slope, I slipped and fell, the camera slammed into a rock(摔在了一块石头上), and I knew right away that it was broken. I felt very depressed and sad. But my parents didn't scold me, and after descending the mountain, my father took it to a local repair shop. Lucky for us, the camera hadn’t broken as badly as I imagined, and the owner repaired the camera again. When I got it back, I felt a sense of joy that it was back in one piece(我感到有种失而复得的欢乐). Since then, I've been more careful with the camera. I always keep it in the box and only take it out when taking photographs. This camera records the happy times for me and my parents and it has become a big part of our family.

So this is something I received for free. / So this is something in my home I broke and then repaired.
answers.summary: 在我十四岁生日的时候，我的父亲在我的生日party上送给了我一台崭新的尼康相机，我感到非常开心，因为我可以用这台相机来记录和表达，但是我在爬山的时候自己摔倒了，把相机也摔坏了，我感到很难过，但父亲找了一家修理店把相机又修好了，我感到有种失而复得的欢乐。

answers.title: 壮志未酬的房子
answers.content:
Although my flat is located in a very ordinary building, there are some houses nearby and I really want to buy one of them. They are actually located in the villa area opposite my apartment. The houses in this villa area are all two or three storeys high. They may also include a basement as part of the property. What I do know is that there are gardens in the front and back of the houses. The rear gardens are especially(后花园) very large. Some owners have decorated their back gardens very beautifully, with gazebos(凉亭), rattan chairs(藤椅) and barbecue grills. Occasionally I can see them grilling in the back garden in the summer, and I think it would be great if I had a house with a garden. Some residents have also dug a swimming pool in their back garden, so they can hold pool parties in the summer. I really envy people who have such houses, and I really want to live a similar house myself.

But of course I don't have enough money to buy one at this stage(现阶段). After all, I have only been working for two years, and the price of these houses is far beyond what I can afford(远远超出了我能承受的范围). Even if I were to work for another five to ten years, at my current salary level I still wouldn’t be able to afford such a villa. However, I am willing to work hard towards this ambition(不过我愿意朝着这个ambition去努力). I will also manage my finances carefully, and try to save enough so that I will be able to make the down payment(首付) earlier. I will also take out a mortgage (申请贷款) in order to pay the remainder. After buying the house, I want to move my whole family in, including my parents and grandparents. Even if I fulfil my ambitions, I will still be saddened by the high prices for such a necessity as a house(我也对于房子这种必需品的超高售价感到很难过). Society is unfair and not everyone who works and lives hard can afford expensive necessities(社会是不公平的，并不是每一个努力工作和生活的人都能负担得起昂贵的必需品).

So this is the ambition that I haven’t achieved. / So this is the house I would like to live in.
answers.summary: 我有一个非常想要的房子，它是......(what where why feel),但是，我还没有攒够足够的钱去购买它，对此，对于这种生活必需品的超高售价，我感到很难过。

answers.title: 浙江余村
answers.content:
Last month I took a holiday to Yucun in Anji county, Zhejiang province. It had just been honored as one of the "Best Tourism Villages" at the World Tourism Organization. I wondered how this small village way off the beaten track(偏远的 had achieved this, so I went there to find out.

The natural scenery there was absolutely gorgeous. However, it is hard to imagine that the green mountains, clear rivers and blue skies of that beautiful village were seriously damaged and shrouded by smog and dust just 20 years ago. Yucun once relied on cement factories and mines. Villagers got rich by selling mineral products but as a result found themselves living in a poor ecological environment. Therefore, the villagers made the decision to conserve the local environment and adjust their development planning. They began to develop leisure eco-tourism including agritainment, homestays and rafting. I lodged in a homestay run by a villager and I was stunned by his methods for promoting his business. He posted photos and videos of the beautiful scenery on his social media account and attracted more visitors to book his rooms.

All the homestays and hotels are well established and comfortably furnished. I felt as if I were staying in a five-star hotel with the most beautiful views outside the window. The days I spent in this little village were so relaxing, thanks to the fresh air and the breathtaking bamboo forest. I retain a deep respect for villagers in Yucun, with their determination and constant efforts in searching for a balance between economic development and protection of the surrounding environment. They did it and it was such a great accomplishment that I should introduce to all those who don’t know the story. From this experience, I have understood that economic development doesn’t have to be achieved at the cost of(以…为代价) destroying the environment. The ecological environment itself is the economy and all environmental protection efforts will be rewarded.
answers.summary: 上个暑假我去了浙江余村，那是一个位于浙江北边的一个小山村。那是一个山峦叠嶂的地方。曾经村民们靠山吃山，毫无限制地开发资源导致环境遭受了破坏。但是现在他们改变了发展策略，通过改良环境发展起了旅游业。今年12月，浙江余村入选联合国首批“世界最佳旅游乡村”名单，他蜕变成了一个很美丽清洁的村落。

answers.title: C罗
answers.content:
Cristiano Ronaldo is a very famous athlete and, I believe, one of the greatest players of all time. I first became aware of this legend(传奇) in football history when I was just a tiny kid. It was a hot summer’s day and I was watching the World Cup with my father at home. Ronaldo is a talented and impressive Portuguese athlete born to play football. Let me point out some of the individual honours he has achieved. He has won FIFA World Player of the year, UEFA Best Player in Europe and the European Golden Boot. Apart from these individual accolades(个人荣誉), his team has won the championship of the English Premier League, UEFA Champions League and FIFA Club World Cup multiple times.

These awards, honours and trophies speak a lot about his greatness. Furthermore, he is also pretty charming and interesting, with a sense of humour. He’s not only a star on the football field, but also a celebrity in the media. There are just so many funny Ronaldo moments posted on YouTube.

During a recent pre-match press conference, he noticed that two bottles of Coca-Cola were sitting directly in front of him. He removed the soft drinks from the view of the cameras and replaced them with a water bottle, rolling his eyes while muttering with an undertone of disgust(不满地嘀咕) "Coca-Cola". Coca-Cola's market value plummeted sharply(大幅下跌) after Ronaldo's antics, and water brands like Evian jumped on this bandwagon to promote their bottled water on Twitter. Even the World Health Organisation called upon people to cut out sugar. He’s just such an interesting character.
answers.summary: 最早和爸爸一起看世界杯认识了他，C罗个活泼开朗，球技精湛。不仅如此，他还很会营销自己，让人们看到他风趣幽默的一面。

answers.title: 学开车
answers.content:
When it comes to a challenging skill to learn, driving is on top of my list(首当其冲). The reason I struggled with(挣扎于……) learning how to drive is that I don’t have a good sense of balance, physically speaking. For years, I avoided learning to drive yet, to be honest, it is an essential skill nowadays. Many of my college friends, on the other hand, passed their driving tests as soon as they graduated from high school. Furthermore, I constantly heard them talking excitedly about taking their parents out for a ride, or running errands(办小事) for them in the car. It then came to me that I was way behind my friends, and I felt really frustrated, because I didn’t want to be looked down upon simply because I could not drive.

As a result, I signed up for some driving lessons in the summer vacation before my senior year. The first test was computer-based, regarding traffic regulations. I studied very hard by taking down notes, working on problem sets, and doing sample tests whenever I had time. Coming up next was the road test, which was much harder for me, considering my poor physical balance. I practiced three times a week in the practice field, which took me 1. 5 hours to arrive at in the scorching hot summer(酷暑). Specifically, I carefully observed my coach and spent more time practicing than anyone else. Despite being frequently criticized for my lack of coordination by my coach, I never gave up. I also searched for videos on various tips to help me pass the test.

Finally, I successfully passed my test before my graduation. It was tough but truly rewarding, giving me more confidence in myself.
answers.summary: 学开车对我来说是一件困难的事情，因为我的四肢协调能力不太好，但我想着当今社会，学车也是一项必备技能。因此在大三的暑假去报名了驾校，最终，我成功地考出了驾照，这个过程是艰难的，但是结果还是可喜的。

answers.title: 希望到新高中后多打扫寝室公共空间
answers.content:
When I was in Grade 11, I was forced to move to a new school because my parents shifted their workplaces from my hometown to a new city. It was then that I got to know Rose, who later on became my friend and helped me adapt to(适应) my new school. The new school was by no means close to my home, so I chose to sleep at school instead of wasting too much time on commuting(通勤) between home and school every day. A dorm of 8 soon became a mess if you didn't do the cleaning for several days.

So, without a mother to rely on, I had to sweep the floor and keep the room tidy on my own. My sense of excitement of escaping from my talkative mother was soon replaced by the troublesome new task of cleaning my dorm. Soon after the semester started, some more good yet burdensome(繁重的) news came, as I was chosen to be the one in charge of our dorm. I was so happy and started to ponder over(深思熟虑) what I could do for my roommates. Days later, I learned that the month of the dorm inspection was approaching. This new school set November as a special month to inspect students’dormitories. Rose, who lived next door, showed me around her dorm which was spotlessly clean. I expressed my concern about the inspection, so she taught me to make it a habit to keep the dorm clean and tidy.

So, I started to clean the public areas of the dorm every day after my evening classes, which impressed and inspired my roommates. Later on, they all joined in with my routine and we lived pretty happily with each other till graduation.
answers.summary: 高二因父母工作关系转学，我住到了套间里，11月我去参观好友rose她们的套间，发现特别干净……我希望自己也能养成这样的好习惯，主动地打扫公共空间，这样能给别的同学留下好印象。

answers.title: 因实习需要买了新电脑
answers.content:
I once made a very hard decision as to whether it was worthwhile(值得的) buying a very expensive computer that cost 10 thousand yuan. It was in my junior year at college. I already had a computer but it worked very slowly because it had been used by my elder sister for several years. Old as it was(尽管很旧), it met my needs for finishing my school papers and searching for information on the Internet.

The story began with my first internship at a studio working as an editorial assistant to produce videos. You might know that it's a very demanding(要求高的) job, because I was required to download certain software and applications(软件应用) on my computer. The old one definitely could not meet this demand(满足需求), so it was necessary for me to buy a new one with a larger memory(内存) to do the job. I went to the store and asked the salesman for some basic information. To my great astonishment, the computer that was compatible with (兼容的) the software was beyond my budget. My budget was 6 thousand yuan, but that computer cost 10 thousand yuan. What bad news for a student who had no way to make money.

I was confronted with a big dilemma(两难的处境). I thought it over for about a week and finally, I decided that I must buy one because this internship was going to be extremely beneficial to me, which would improve my skill of film editing and provide me with valuable experience in journalism. I asked my parents for another 1000 yuan and then used my scholarship to buy the new computer. The decision was hard to make but the result was unexpectedly great.
answers.summary: 大三时我买了新的电脑，花了一万左右。因为当时我需要剪辑视频，要有好配置的电脑，没想到一台适合剪辑片子的电脑要这么贵，我的预算是6000左右。这实在让我有些犹豫，这笔花费对一个没有收入的学生来说，实在是有点多了……最后我还是下决心买了电脑。

answers.title: 给室友带饭每次都要等很久
answers.content:
I feel very conflicted about (感到纠结...) whether I should say no to my roommate. She is kind of a lazy girl because she always asks me to bring food for supper every day. It’s hard to refuse because she sleeps just below my bed and we share the same desk and many other things. After all, she is not bad in other aspects.

This girl doesn’t get along well with us as she hardly joins us when we’re playing or eating outside, so we just keep a very polite relationship. But to be honest, it’s quite annoying bringing her food every day as it takes up(占用) some of my time. Let me put it this way - I could have this time at my own disposal(自由支配), but since I have to bring her food back to the dormitory, I spend a lot of time commuting(来回路程).

For example, it was before the mid-term examination that I felt the most irritated by(惹恼) her. I was so busy preparing for the test that I just had a quick meal every day, but she still kept asking me to buy food for her. One day when I called her up urgently at the canteen, there was no answer. I got so furious because people lining up behind me kept pushing me to order. Later she explained that she was taking a shower, so I could not really blame her for that. I think I won’t say no to her for the rest of my school days for the sake of (为……考虑) our relationship. .
answers.summary: 我的下铺室友常让我帮她带晚饭，这让我觉得很讨厌，我本来可以自己安排自己的时间，我印象最深的在我期中考前赶着复习时，她还要让我帮忙带饭。但作为室友，我想我可能还会继续友善地对待她。

answers.title: 我做志愿者早起迎接Airbnb老总来演讲
answers.content:
I remember that it was in my freshman year when I saw a special volunteer recruitment post on the school website. Brian Chesky, the founder and CEO of Airbnb, was coming to China to give a lecture at our university! The school was recruiting volunteers for this event. It was like a huge surprise for me! I was over the moon(欣喜若狂), and signed up for this opportunity immediately.

Brian Chesky is a businessman I've always admired. I have read about him a lot in the financial press. He is the one who created the concept of shared space, where people are expected to make the most of their free space along with others, providing travelers with a unique travel experience. His ideas spread all over the world(全世界). Almost every time I travel, I book b&bs using Airbnb. On this platform, there are many places with amazing local characteristics, which provide me with a warmer and more comfortable living atmosphere compared to that of hotels.

On the event day, I got up early because I had to get trained before the lecture. I waited excitedly for his arrival at the entrance without feeling sleepy at all. The wait was long, exciting but a little bit tiring because I had to keep my posture(保持一个姿势) for a long time as a volunteer to welcome him. However, thinking of meeting Brian in person(亲眼见到), I became excited and energetic again.

Finally, his lecture was simply amazing. He vividly shared his entrepreneurial experience. From his story, I realized that he is a very discerning man who is good at discovering opportunities, starting from solving small problems to helping him achieve big success.
answers.summary: 我记得大一时，我早去做志愿者，因为崇拜的Airbnb老总要来演讲，等待的过程是漫长疲劳又激动人心的，我听自己的偶像在现场生动地分享自己的创业经历，他真的是一个非常有洞察力的人，从身边的小问题出发，取得了大成功。

answers.title: 自驾去稻城
answers.content:
Last summer vacation I was supposed to(打算，计划) take a road trip to Daocheng, a county of western Sichuan Province, with one of my friends. It was his idea but something unexpected came up which meant he had to pull out(临时有事). I’d spent a long time preparing, and didn’t want to change my plans(旅程) at the last minute, so I decided to just go alone.

Actually it was the first time I’d taken a road trip by myself and I knew it could be a little dicey(危险的) as the destination is located in the eastern Hengduan Mountains where the terrain is extremely treacherous(崎岖的，不平的). This, of course, made the route more dangerous and complicated than most road trip destinations. Once I’d set off, things were ok, but sometimes I fretted over(焦虑，担忧) taking a wrong turn and getting lost. I needn’t have worried though, as with the help of the car’s internal navigation system, I reached Daocheng county early in the morning and was able to see the pre-sunrise glow. My fatigue and fear completely vanished as I saw the first rays of sunlight spreading in the East. Standing on top of a mountain and looking down at the paddy fields(稻田) in the beautiful valley with the wind blowing through(吹拂) the green crops, I felt the sun on my skin and felt so alive and refreshed.

I have to admit that I was kind of disappointed when I’d heard that I’d have to travel alone by car, but now I feel fortunate that because of my perseverance I got to see the breathtaking scenery and stunning sights(美景).
answers.summary: 去年暑假与朋友相约自驾游，朋友因为临时有事没去，我最终一个人坚持前往。一个人自驾游对我来说是一次冒险，但我还是利用导航和地图抵达了目的地，还早起欣赏了晨晖中的稻城。很庆幸没有放弃这次旅行，才能看到壮丽的美景。

topics.title: 为家人骄傲/（自行车/摩托车/）汽车旅行/想学的感兴趣的技能

answers.title: 祖父去自驾游让我想学车
answers.content:
One day in my freshman year, I received a letter sent by my grandpa. I opened it up and there was a photo inside from which I discovered that he’d taken a short road trip and found a perfect place for fishing just outside of our city. It reminded me of(使记起，使想起) a time, several months prior, when he’d told me that he was driving around looking for new fishing spots. I’d called him fairly quickly and learned that he was fishing with some local people in a pond. He told me not to worry about him since he was prepared and had planned his itinerary(旅程，行程) carefully in advance(提前) . As well as that, he’d also used a mapping app for directions. He told me some of the things that had happened along the way, and encouraged me to learn how to drive so that next time we could travel together. I was quite interested(令人感兴趣的) in joining him, and felt so proud of him since, though he was old, he was still strong and mobile(老当益壮).

My grandpa’s story was nothing short of inspiring(鼓舞人心的) to me. Under his influence, I couldn’t help but think that traveling by car is something really interesting which I had to try. So I signed up(报名参加) to join a driving school and also looked for a reliable(靠谱的) freelance driving instructor so that I could spend the whole winter vacation learning. I managed to get my driving license early the following year and was really excited about taking a road trip and going camping with my young-at-heart grandpa.
answers.summary: 在大一的某一天，祖父自驾去了城市周边钓鱼，真是老当益壮，我很敬佩他，这让我觉得自己开车旅行竟然是那么有趣的事情，我觉得自己也不能落后，应该赶紧学车，希望能在明年考出驾照，和他一起自驾去城市周边露营。

Describe a person who solved a problem in a smart way

answers.title: 美术课上同桌把墨点变成小鸟
answers.content:
When we were in primary school, we would switch desk mates (同桌) every semester. In the third grade, I shared the same table with Kate, the class commissary in charge of art (美术委员). I happened to know her from the first art class when her work was displayed as an example for us to learn from. My friend told me that her family had discovered her talent of painting and had cultivated this hobby since she was two or three years old. When we were overwhelmed by (不知所措) the class task of painting a picture with watercolor, she had already mastered it, together with Chinese paintings, oil paintings and many other kinds.

What impressed me most was that during one class, when the teacher taught us watercolor painting (水墨画), I was poor at controlling the brush, so I accidentally dropped a large spot of paint on the rice paper (宣纸) and the whole picture was ruined. At the time, the class was almost over when the art teacher urged us to hand in our work, so it would have been too late to draw a new one. Kate saw that I was very anxious, so she approached me and discovered the stain on my picture. She immediately picked up the brush and turned the black dot into a flying bird in some simple strokes with her outstanding artistic skills. Her kind help not only saved me (免去) the trouble of painting a new one, but also made my picture more vivid.

Since then, she has been a model for me for her kindness and excellence, and I have often imitated her style of painting in order to become as creative as she is.
answers.summary: 我的同桌Kate很会画画，有一节课老师教我们画水墨画，我把墨水滴到了宣纸上，我的同桌发现了我焦躁万分，他凑过来看到了我画面上的污点，拿起画笔机智地把墨点变成了一只小鸟，我不禁感叹他一个又机智又有创造力的男孩！

Describe a creative person whose work you admire

answers.title: 凡尔纳和他的《海底两万里》
answers.content:
A good book is undoubtedly the best companion for a man, so today, I’d like to take this opportunity to talk about a sensational piece of literature(轰动一时的文学作品) that I found particularly gratifying. Basically, I am a bit of a bookworm, so I’ve built up a nice collection of books, however, “Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea”, written by the French author Jules Verne, is my most favorite of all time.
 .
Verne’s aforementioned novel was introduced to us in Chinese class when I was in the fourth grade of elementary school, and I was so taken by it that I have read the book over and over(多次，反复) ever since. It is both a science fiction and adventure novel and tells the story of Captain Nemo and his "uninvited guests" traveling under the sea for ten months on the Nautilus, depicting(描绘) their interesting marine life. In the Nautilus, people wear silk suits made of shellfish, are sprayed with perfume brewed from marine plants, and sleep on a bed made of large algae leaves.
 .
Every time I go back to the book, I am astonished by his imagination. I remember first reading it as a child and wanting to dive to the bottom of the sea to check it out(一探究竟). Moreover, closely related to his reputation as a science-fiction author is the often-repeated claim that he is a "prophet" of scientific progress, and that many of his novels involve elements of technology that were ahead of their time and later became commonplace. The submarine described in the book finally became a reality! Besides, he also envisioned the possibility(展望…的可能性) of aircraft carriers and helicopters in other works. I can’t help but(禁不住) wonder whether Verne really had the ability to predict the future. He devoted his life to creating science fiction and adventure novels, and I admire his insight very much.
answers.summary: 小学四年级时我在课堂上接触到凡尔纳，他的《海底两万里》，这是一本科学幻想冒险小说，我每次阅读都会惊讶于他的想象力，凡尔纳一生致力于创作科幻历险小说，我很敬佩他的脑洞。

questions.content: Describe a good service you received Describe a café you like to visit

answers.title: Tims咖啡馆的优质服务
answers.content:
Well, speaking of good service, I want to talk about the service in a café called Tim’s where I often study with my friends on weekends. It is within walking distance of (离…步行距离) our college and decorated in a Scandinavian style (装修成北欧风格) with some modern abstract paintings on the wall. I mostly like sitting at the bar and smelling the aroma of (香气) coffee beans, which provides a healing feeling and even improves my concentration levels. Moreover, Tim’s is also a good place for group discussions with classmates since the environment is relaxing and comfortable. As well as selling coffee, they also offer teas, healthy fruit juices and some dainty (美味的) cupcakes, which all taste wonderful.

What makes me a regular visitor (常客) is the service they provide. The waiters greet the customers warmly every time and serve the food quickly. In addition, since there are so many students who go there to study, the café offers power points so that they can charge mobiles and laptops for free, which is very considerate.

What impressed me most was when I went there once on a rainy day to study by myself. When I was about to scan the code to pay for my drink, I found that I had left my phone in the dormitory. I was really embarrassed, but the waiter not only lent me an umbrella to go back and get it, but also told me he would wait until I came back to pay. When I returned, the hot cappuccino I usually order was waiting for me on the table, which made me feel so warm and welcome. Because of the kind way they handled my forgetfulness, I have a really high opinion of Tim’s, which is why I often recommend it to my friends.
answers.summary: 我常在周末和朋友相约在Tims 咖啡馆自习、小组讨论。除了售卖咖啡外，Tims还提供牛油果、芒果等果饮和一些精巧的小蛋糕。服务生很热情，还会免费给我们提供给手机和电脑充电的充电宝。特别有一次他们在我忘带钱时，还为我先上了咖啡、借了我雨伞，因为这件事，我对Tims的印象就更好了。

answers.title: 在网上买了本健康杂志分享给朋友
answers.content:
When I was shopping online last week, I happened to come to a store that specialized in second-hand books and magazines. This store was full of second-hand bookstores, with all kinds of books, comics, magazines, new and old, and even many books that were out of print(绝版书). I am a person who likes to read very much, so I wandered around.

While browsing, I saw a magazine called "Health World". What attracted me was the title on its cover --- "How to Improve Sleep Quality". My friend Emily had recently been suffering from insomnia(失眠), and she often complained to me in the middle of the night. She tried to find a professional doctor and took sleeping pills, but her quality of sleep was still not improving. After reading this article, I felt that its methods to solve insomnia mentioned in it looked very reliable and should be of great help to Emily. For example, it mentioned in the article that you need to adopt a comfortable sleeping position when sleeping. We may feel that it is impossible to control our sleeping posture when we sleep because we will be in a state of unconsciousness(无意识状态). In fact, this can be done using the methods mentioned in the article. You can also use meditation(冥想) to help sleep. You can think about things that make you happy, or things that you want to do before sleep.

So, I bought this magazine and couldn't wait to share it with Emily. Just yesterday, Emily told me that the method from the article was highly effective. I am very happy that by sharing this I could help her. At the same time, I also think the professionalism of this article is worthy of recognition(这篇文章的专业性是值得肯定的).
answers.summary:
这商店有各类图书，我看到了一本叫《健康世界》的杂志，它封面上的标题—“如何提高睡眠质量”。因为我的朋友Emily最近被失眠所困扰，经常找我抱怨。于是我买下了这本书并迫不及待地分享给了Emily。
Specialize – 专营
Second-hand – 二手的
Out of print – 绝版的
Insomnia – 失眠
Reliable – 可靠的
Posture – 姿势
Unconsciousness – 无意识
Meditation – 冥想
Professionalism – 专业性

answers.title: 海边沙滩足球比赛
answers.content:
I like to play football with my friends after school. We play football on the school playground every afternoon. Once, by chance, when we were walking along the beach, we saw some people playing football on the beach. I had seen beach volleyball before, but this was the first time I saw beach football. This game quickly attracted our attention (吸引…的注意) . Everyone thought beach football was very interesting, so we also decided to have a beach football match.

Before kicking the ball, we needed to prepare special beach soccer shoes to prevent our feet from sinking into(沉入) the sand during running and jumping. In addition, we also prepared some sun protection clothes and sun cream. After everything was ready, we found an empty beach to have the game. Since I have played football on land before, I think beach football is not difficult, but you need to be careful not to fall down while running; after all, the sand is soft. The second thing is that the sand will be easily thrown around (沙很容易被扬起来) when playing football so you also need to be careful not to get sand in your eyes. We were divided into two teams. The game was very fierce(激烈的), but everyone had a great time playing, and everyone’s faces were filled with happy smiles.

After this game, I fell in love with beach football. This is not a common sport, but it is a very interesting one. Whether or not we won or lost the game, we all enjoyed the moments of playing with our friends. If I had the chance, I would like to try this leisure activity near the seaside again.
answers.summary: 在大学毕业旅行的时候，我和我的朋友们去了青岛玩。我们在海边玩耍时，看到有人在沙滩上踢足球，我们觉得很有趣，于是也想来一场沙滩足球比赛。在踢球前，我们需要准备专用的沙滩足球鞋，以及一些防晒的衣物。一切准备就绪后，我们便开始了比赛。由于之前有踢过陆地上的足球，所以我认为沙滩足球也并没有很难。我们几个人分成了两队，比赛非常的焦灼，但大家都玩的不亦乐乎，每个人的脸上都洋溢着开心的笑容。无论比赛输赢，我们都很享受和朋友们在一起玩的时刻。

answers.title: 喜欢的老演员令人尊敬
answers.content:
My favorite actor is Jackie Chan. Jackie Chan is a famous and influential celebrity(名人). In addition to being an actor, Jackie Chan is also a successful screenwriter, director, producer, singer and philanthropist(慈善家). I’ve known about him since I was a kid from watching the movie "Rush Hour" with my dad. Jackie Chan is a (武术) dedicated(敬业的) martial arts actor. In almost all of his movies, he insists on(坚持) not using a stuntman(替身) for any complicated Kung Fu moves. For this reason, he has suffered a lot of injuries, and several times he even put his life in danger(把生命置于危险中). This is the reason why I admire him very much, because he has taught people all over the world about Chinese Kung Fu and the professionalism(敬业精神) of Chinese people. After watching Jackie Chan's movies, I began to have a strong interest in martial arts.

Last year, I was particularly fortunate to attend his new film conference(电影发布会) and had a brief exchange(有短暂的交流) with him. I expressed to him that I wanted to learn Kung Fu. To my surprise, Jackie Chan was very supportive. He said that he regards Chinese Kung Fu as his lifelong career(毕生职业), and hopes to see more people become interested in Kung Fu. During the conversation, he was very amiable(亲切友好的) and there was no sense of distance(没有距离感) between us.

The reason I particularly like and respect him is that he is very dedicated to his work, he is keen on(热衷于) donating to charity and has a great sense of social responsibility(社会责任感). He tries his best to help everyone in need(每个需要帮助的人), and he is highly praised (在社会上获得很高的评价) for this reason. I plan to put learning martial arts on my agenda(把…提上日程). I am going to enroll in a martial arts class, and I will practice hard. If I can learn Kung Fu, I will be very happy!
answers.summary: 我特别喜欢演员成龙，我是小时候和爸爸一起看电影《尖峰时刻》知道的他。他是一位武打演员，非常的敬业，很多高难度的武打电影他都不用替身，亲自上场，所以我也非常敬佩他。从那时起，我就对武术产生了浓厚的兴趣。去年，我特别幸运地参加了他的电影发布会并于他有过短暂的交流，我向他表达了我想学习武术的意愿，他非常的支持我，在交流中我觉得他非常的和蔼可亲，没有距离感。我计划接下来把学习武术提上日程。我准备自己报一个武术班，平时自己也勤加练习。如果能够学会，我会感到非常开心！

answers.title: 在社交媒体上看到有趣的做手工视频但不太成功
answers.content:
In fact, this happened the day before yesterday. I watched a short video on social media about folding paper into a little jumping frog. The folded little jumping frog could bounce(弹跳) remarkably high and far, and it seemed from the video that the process of making the paper frog was not too difficult. I thought it was worth trying and should be interesting, so I wanted to make one myself.

First, I found a rectangular piece of stiff paper. In order to make it more like a frog, I also colored this piece of paper brownish-green(棕绿色) with a colored pencil. Next, I followed the video step by step to fold the frog. This was really a meticulous job(这可真是一个细致的活儿). In order for the paper frog to bounce, the folding requirements were very strict, and some steps were even accurate to the millimeters(有些步骤甚至精确到了毫米). I finally finished it, but the end product did not look like a frog, and it couldn't jump as far as in the video. I folded a few more paper frogs, but none of them were successful.

I think that handicraft work may need personal guidance after all, the steps in all kinds of teaching videos are not that detailed, and some of the steps seem quite complicated. Although the result was not very successful, I enjoyed the process of making it. I think this handmade craft has exercised my creative abilities(动手能力), and I also had fun while folding it.
answers.summary: 前天我在社交媒体上刷到了一个关于用纸折小跳蛙的短视频，折好的小跳蛙可以弹的特别高特别远，我觉得特别有趣，所以我也想自己做一个。我拿出一张长方形的硬纸，跟着视频的步骤一步一步模仿折叠，但是最后折好的成果看起来并不像一只青蛙，而且也不能像视频里那样跳很远。我觉得做手工可能还是需要有人亲自指导，视频中的步骤并没有那么详细。虽然结果不太成功，但是做的过程我很开心，锻炼了我的动手能力。

answers.title: 图书馆很安静，禁止使用手机
answers.content:
I like to go to the library in the city to read. Every time I need to write an essay or when I am bored, I take my laptop there because the city library is a very quiet place and there are many professional books(专业书) that the university library does not have. My friend took me to the city library for the first time. After that, I would go there every weekend to read books that interest me. The city library stipulates(规定) that mobile phones cannot be used, so everyone is reading and studying quietly. But when I went there for the first time, I didn't know this rule. After reading a book for a while, I took out my phone to play. After a while, a librarian came over and told me this rule, so I immediately apologized and put my phone back in my bag.

At the beginning, I was not used to not having my mobile phone in my hand, so I always wanted to look at my phone. But then when I was completely immersed in(沉浸在) a book, my dependence on(对…的依赖) mobile phones was forgotten. I found that without the interruption of my mobile phone, I was able to focus more on studying and reading, and my writing speed increased a lot. So, because of this, I fell in love with going to the city library to study or read. Although the use of mobile phones is not allowed there, it is worth it because I get to study and read more efficiently.
answers.summary: 我特别喜欢去市里的图书馆里看书，那里特别安静。第一次去市图书馆是我朋友带我去的，之后我每周末都会去那里阅读我感兴趣的书籍。图书馆规定不能使用手机，每个人都在安静的读书和学习。一开始没有手机我还不太习惯，但后来我完全沉浸在书中的时候，我也就忘掉了自己对手机的依赖。没有了手机的打扰，我也能够更加专注于学习，所以我爱上了去图书馆读书。

answers.title: 当地新闻出台新的环保法律
answers.content:
Last month, my city issued a new law on garbage classification(垃圾分类), which was updated in the "Environmental Protection Law of the People's Republic of China". The first time I saw this new regulation was on our local news via a WeChat official account. The article detailed(详细讲述了) the rules on how to classify garbage, and also explained the penalties that would be issued if the garbage classification is not carried out in accordance with the law(没有按照法规执行).

In this law, it is stipulated(规定) in detail that domestic garbage will be classified according to the standards of recyclables, hazardous garbage, wet garbage and dry garbage(可回收垃圾，有害垃圾，湿垃圾，干垃圾). Recyclables refer to domestic waste that is suitable for recycling, such as paper, plastics, glass products, metals and fabrics. Hazardous waste refers to domestic waste that can cause direct or potential harm to human health or the natural environment, such as batteries, medicines, paint and its container. Wet garbage refers to perishable(易腐烂的) garbage, such as food waste, leftovers, expired food, flowers and green plants. Dry garbage refers to other household waste, except recyclables, hazardous garbage, and wet garbage. If the garbage is not recycled in accordance with the regulations and the person refuses to make corrections, a fine of no less than 500 Yuan but no more than 5, 000 Yuan shall be imposed(被强制执行).

We are all very interested in this new law because it is related to everyone’s daily life. I think this is a very good law. If garbage classification can be implemented well, every one of us will benefit.
answers.summary: 最近我的城市新出台了一项关于垃圾分类的法律法规，这项法规更新在《中华人民共和国环境保护法》中。我第一次听到这个新的法规是在我们当地的新闻公众号上，公众号推文详细介绍了关于垃圾如何分类的细则，也说明了如果不按规定分类的处罚措施。我们都对这个新的规定很感兴趣，因为它关系到我们每个人的日常生活。我认为这是一个很好的法律，如果垃圾分类能够被很好的执行，受益的将是我们每一个人。

topics.title: （保留）想生活的城市/雇佣很多员工的公司/高楼

questions.content: Describe a town or city where you would like to live in the future Describe a company where you live that employs a lot people Describe a tall building in your city you like or dislike

source_payload.title: （保留）想生活的城市/雇佣很多员工的公司/高楼

sub_questions.content:
Describe a town or city where you would like to live in the future
Describe a company where you live that employs a lot people
Describe a tall building in your city you like or dislike

answers.title: 上海的高楼上有大公司
answers.content:
I want to live in Shanghai in the future. I went to university in Shanghai and lived there for four years. The most famous place in Shanghai is Lujiazui, which has the headquarters of many of the multinational banks of China and East Asia, and is one of the most influential financial centers in China. There are many high-rise buildings(高楼大厦) there, and one of my favorite tall buildings is the Shanghai World Financial Center. It is an office building, 492 meters high with 101 floors above ground and 3 floors underground. The strangest thing about this high-rise building is that it looks very much like a corkscrew(开瓶器) from the outside.

I like this tall building very much, not only because of its unique shape, but also because of the company Ernst & Young. Ernst & Young is the world's leading(顶尖的) professional services company and one of the world's fourth largest accounting firms. It provides auditing, taxation and financial transaction consulting services(审计，税务和金融交易资讯服务) and has been in business for more than 100 years. Ernst & Young has thousands of employees in Shanghai. The employees inside are all elites(精英) of the industry. I was lucky enough to be an intern there when I was in my senior year, and I felt that it was very professional while also being a dynamic(有活力的) company.

I want to work in this company after my graduation, which is also the main reason why I want to live in Shanghai.
answers.summary: 我以后想在上海生活，因为我大学在上海，因此也了解了上海。上海最著名的就是陆家嘴了，那儿有很多高楼大厦。其中有一栋楼是上海环球金融中心，是一栋办公楼，高492米，从外表看上去非常像一个开瓶器。我很喜欢这栋楼，不光是因为它造型独特，更重要的是这栋楼里有我喜欢的企业安永会计师事务所。安永有数千名员工，主要负责审计、税务、咨询等工作。我曾经在那里做过实习生，我觉得它是一家非常专业并且有活力的公司。我想进入这家公司工作，这也是我想住在上海生活的原因。

topics.title: （保留）朋友争执/喜欢的电影/帮助朋友

questions.content: Describe an argument two of your friends had Describe a movie that you like Describe a time when you helped a friend

source_payload.title: （保留）朋友争执/喜欢的电影/帮助朋友

sub_questions.content:
Describe an argument two of your friends had
Describe a movie that you like
Describe a time when you helped a friend

answers.title: 帮朋友解决看电影时的争执
answers.content:
During Chinese New Year , myself and all my high school classmates returned to our hometown. Of course, I went out to party with my good friends. My two best friends, Mary and Lily, and I went to the cinema to watch a movie that was just released(上映) at the time, called "Hello, Li Huanying". Although the movie had just been released, it had a great reputation, and it was trending online.

I liked this movie very much. It tells the story of a girl who accidentally traveled back to 20 years ago and met her mother, Li Huanying, when she was younger. This movie was extremely funny because it was co-starred(联合出演) by some of the most famous comedians in China. We laughed constantly whilst watching the movie. At the same time, the great maternal love(伟大的母爱) shown in the movie's ending was also moving. My friends and I all had tears in our eyes at the end. This is a very good movie. It is funny but also celebrates maternal love, so I like it very much.

While we were watching the movie, Mary accidentally knocked over(掀翻) Lily's drink, and they started to argue. In order to not disturb other people watching the movie, I tried my best to calm them down and bought them two new drinks. After a while, both reconciled(和好) and apologized to me. Personally, I feel that friends should not quarrel about such trivial matters(如此琐碎的小事). I am happy to help them resolve their conflicts and maintain the group’s friendship.
answers.summary: 今年过年期间我和我的两个朋友Mary和Lily去电影院看了一部电影《你好李焕英》，我很喜欢这部电影，它讲述了一个女孩意外穿越回20年前，与正值青春的母亲李焕英相遇的故事。这部电影特别欢乐，同时，结局展现出的伟大母爱也令人感动，所以我很喜欢它。但是在看电影的过程中，Mary不小心打翻了Lily的饮料，她们因此产生了争执。为了不在观影时影响他人，我极力劝说她们，并重新买了两杯饮料给她们，冷静之后她们两也和好了。我觉得朋友之间不应该因为这种小事而争吵，我很高兴帮助他们解决了矛盾，维护了我们的友谊。

topics.title: （保留）智力游戏/想提升的天赋/生活计划

questions.content: Describe a puzzle you have played(Jigsaw, Crossword, etc.) Describe a natural talent (sports, music, etc) you want to improve

source_payload.title: （保留）智力游戏/想提升的天赋/生活计划

sub_questions.content:
Describe a puzzle you have played(Jigsaw, Crossword, etc.)
Describe a natural talent (sports, music, etc) you want to improve

answers.title: 计划通过英语智力游戏提高英语语言天赋
answers.content:
I have been gifted in (有…的天赋) learning the English language since I was a child, because I could remember many words easily and quickly. In order to better improve my English fluency , I plan to buy several English crossword puzzles.

In the past , I used to do the crossword puzzles in newspapers. I remember that for one I played, I needed to fill out 15 words based on the clues given. However, my vocabulary was relatively limited at the time, and I could not understand some of the clues, so it took me roughly an hour to look up(查阅) a lot of words in the dictionary to complete the crossword . Despite this, I didn't find this game tiring, on the contrary, I fell in love with this game! In the process of filling it in, I not only learned some interesting knowledge, but also, since I needed to look up things in the dictionary frequently, I remembered lots of words. I think this game can help to increase my English vocabulary greatly, and the process of completing a crossword is also interesting. Whenever I solve a puzzle, I feel a sense of accomplishment(成就感)! After completing the crossword puzzle in the newspaper, I absolutely fell in love with them. So, I plan to go to the bookstore to buy more English books that specialize in word-filling games(专门用来玩填字游戏的英文书), so that I can improve my English language easily by just playing，which is really a bonus of the game(游戏带来的额外红利). If I can improve my English talent through the crossword puzzle games, I will feel incredibly happy.
answers.summary: 我从小就对学习英语有天赋，很轻松就能记住很多单词。为了更好的提高我的语言天赋，我计划买几套英语填字游戏。我曾经在报纸上做过填字游戏，需要填出15个单词。当时我词汇量比较低，所以我花了一个小时才填完，但我并没有觉得这个游戏很难。在填的过程中，我需要频繁的查字典，我认为这个游戏能够很好的增加我的词汇量，同时填字的过程也非常有趣，每当我填好一份时，我都非常的有成就感！如果通过这个有趣的游戏提高了我的英语天赋，我会感到非常开心。

topics.title: 拜访过但不想住的家/不喜欢的短途旅行

questions.content: Describe a home that you visited but did not want to live in Describe a short trip you often take but do not like

source_payload.title: 拜访过但不想住的家/不喜欢的短途旅行

sub_questions.content:
Describe a home that you visited but did not want to live in
Describe a short trip you often take but do not like

answers.title: 最讨厌每周去住在垃圾站旁边的英语老师家补课
answers.content:
When I was in senior school, I used to take the bus to my English teacher Mrs Zhao’s home to take tutoring (家教) lessons. Her house was right next to a big garbage collection, so you can imagine how smelly(难闻的) and dirty it was. Although her home was clean and tidy because she put everything in order, I couldn’t stand looking out of the windows and seeing garbage scattered about(随意丢弃).

What’s more, Mrs Zhao arranged our class in the suburbs where she spends time during the weekend. It’s about 10 miles from the city center, so it took me about 30 minutes to get there by bus, which made me feel sick because of the bumpy (颠簸) road along the way. There were many times when I felt like giving up studying with her, but my parents always encouraged me and told me that if I want to be the best, I need to be able to withstand the bitterest of the bitter(吃得苦中苦). It was that belief that kept me studying every weekend during that semester(学期).

However, if it was up to me, I wouldn’t have gone to Mrs Zhao’s home even if she taught me with full passion, simply because I couldn’t bear the environment and the uncomfortable trip.
answers.summary: 高中时每周末都要去英语老师家补课，去她家的公交非常颠簸总会让我感到不舒服。她家挨着一个垃圾回收站。你可以想象那附近的环境有多差，多脏。她家倒是收拾得很干净，但是往窗户外一看，就看到了遍地的垃圾。不管这样的房子住着有多舒服，我都不愿意住这样的房子。

topics.title: 喜欢的天气/喜欢的城镇区域

questions.content: Describe a kind of weather you like Describe a part of a city or town you enjoy spending time in

source_payload.title: 喜欢的天气/喜欢的城镇区域

sub_questions.content:
Describe a kind of weather you like
Describe a part of a city or town you enjoy spending time in

answers.title: 喜欢晴天去周庄漫步
answers.content:
Although I’m not a British native, I like to talk about the weather. Among all kinds of weather, I like sunny days best. And to tell you the truth, I used to name myself Sunny several years ago because I liked to see the sun above the sky and feel the warmth and power. Compared with (对比) rainy or windy weather, sunny days always make life more enriched(充实的) and colourful. I like them also for the reason that(因为) I enjoy taking a walk outdoors. I like ZhouZhuang in particular because it’s a town combining(结合) modern facilities with old-fashioned culture and lifestyle.

Zhouzhuang is a famous tourist attraction which is well known for its charming scenery and well-preserved(保存完好的) old lifestyle. It’s also called the Venice of China. So now, you should have a rough(大致的) understanding about it. I like walking on the cobblestone(鹅卵石) roads, especially on sunny days when the old architecture is bathing in the sun(沐浴在日光下), looking shiny and pleasing. Last but not least, I like staying there not only because of its scenery but also due to the relaxing way of life. Unlike a metropolis(大都市), there’s no hustle and bustle(喧嚣) in Zhouzhuang, making it quiet and peaceful. I have time and space to meditate(冥想) without being bothered by any other things. I found my true self, so I really enjoy spending time there.
answers.summary: 我最喜欢的天气是晴天。因为晴天时的太阳总会带给我温暖和力量。晴天时，我总喜欢去周庄古镇漫步，周庄晴天时也非常的美丽，太阳照在众多古建筑上，让它们熠熠发光。我之所以喜欢周庄是因为它慢生活的生活氛围，在周庄漫步，我总能感受到内心的宁静。

topics.title: 电脑罢工/告诉朋友实情（低频）

questions.content: Describe a time when your computer broke down Describe a time when you told your friend an important truth

source_payload.title: 电脑罢工/告诉朋友实情（低频）

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time when your computer broke down
Describe a time when you told your friend an important truth

answers.title: 告诉朋友自己从那借的电脑坏了
answers.content:
Because of the epidemic, all the classes in our school have been moved online. Therefore, a laptop has become a must for all students. However, my laptop is still in my dormitory because I didn’t take it home with me, so I had to borrow my good friend Kathy’s laptop.

Last Wednesday, I was taking an online class using her laptop when all of a sudden(突然之间), the screen went black. No matter which key I pressed, the laptop didn’t respond. Even restarting couldn’t help. I was worried and rattled(慌乱的) because Kathy always took good care of it. She even bought a protective case for it because she didn’t want any scratches(划痕) on it. I couldn’t imagine what her reaction would be if she found out it was broken. I rushed out of the door and found a repair center for help, who were able to fix it. It was almost 11 o’clock at night by the time I got it back but I was thrilled that they managed to fix it.

However, I felt guilty that I didn’t keep an eye on the condition of the laptop. Although it was working again, I had to tell Kathy about the incident instead of pretending nothing happened.
Fortunately, she didn’t go mad (生气) when I told her and instead she comforted me by saying it wasn’t my fault. She also told me that she appreciated how honest and responsible I was. I believe honesty is the most important quality between friends, so I’m happy I told her the truth and I’m proud of myself for doing so.
answers.summary:
这次疫情后,我们学校所有的课都改成网课了。但是我的电脑放在了学校宿舍没有
带回家,于是我向好友 Kathy借了她的电脑上网课。上周三,我在家用电脑上学校的网课,突然电脑就黑屏了。我很着急,因为 Kathy一直都很宝贝自己的电脑。我觉得很内疚懊恼,感觉自己没有好好保护电脑。最终我还是决定告诉Katy她
宝贝的电脑突然出故障,但是我去店铺修好了。她没有生气,反而安慰我这不是我的错。
我觉得朋友之间诚实是最重要的品质,所以我一定要告诉她真相。

topics.title: 许下的承诺/对家庭重要的东西（保留）

questions.content: Describe a time you made a promise to someone Describe something important that has been kept in your family for a long time

source_payload.title: 许下的承诺/对家庭重要的东西（保留）

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time you made a promise to someone
Describe something important that has been kept in your family for a long time

answers.title: 之前对妈妈承诺实习后要给家里买洗碗机
answers.content:
Promises have a special meaning to me, because my mom has always told me to keep promises whatever I do. So upon seeing this kind of topic, I couldn’t help thinking about an old promise between my mom and I.

I graduated from college three years ago and started working in a large consulting (咨询) company as an intern(实习生). It was my first job, which meant that I would get my first pot of gold. Considering my mom had complained about being tired of washing dishes every day, I was determined to do something to help. So, as soon as I got to work, I told my mom that I would buy her a dishwasher on my first payday, knowing that she wanted one for a long time but felt reluctant (不愿意) to buy one.

She responded with a large smile, assuming(认为) I was just kidding. Soon, payday arrived and to my great astonishment, I earned twice as much as expected because of a bonus(奖金). I went wild with joy(欣喜若狂) because I could afford to buy a dishwasher. Later that day, I took mom to buy one and she was moved to tears because she never actually thought I could keep my promise, and I felt that nothing could be better than that moment. We’ve had it for three years now and my mom has been set free from washing dishes every day, so she feels more relaxed. We consider it one of our most essential(必备的) appliances.
answers.summary: 在我刚实习时，我就许诺妈妈第一个月的工资一定要给她买她想要很久的洗碗机，为了让她可以从此解放双手不用再为洗碗头疼。由于是实习工资比较丰厚，我很轻松顺利的完成了自己的许诺。这个洗碗机转眼间已经陪伴我们家三年啦，是我们家不可或缺的重要设施。

questions.content: 现场体育比赛/教别人的技能/看过的体育比赛/喜欢的节目/想看的体育赛事/经常登录的网站Describe a sports event you would like to watch Describe a sports competition you watched. Describe a website you often visit

source_payload.title: 现场体育比赛/教别人的技能/看过的体育比赛/喜欢的节目/想看的体育赛事/经常登录的网站

sub_questions.content:
现场体育比赛/教别人的技能/看过的体育比赛/喜欢的节目/想看的体育赛事/经常登录的网站Describe a sports event you would like to watch
Describe a sports competition you watched.
Describe a website you often visit

answers.title: 被排球赛吸引以致于可以教别人打排球
answers.content:
Two years ago, I watched a live volleyball match in the stadium of my city. To be honest, the atmosphere was quite good. Players hugged each other before the match and they were encouraging each other(加油打气) during it. The ball rose, traveled and fell, which was quite magnificent(壮观的).

Because of this match, my interest in playing volleyball was piqued(被激发起对......的兴趣), so I joined the volleyball club at school. I was eager to be a good player so I practiced every day. After two years’ practice, I was able to play very well. On weekends(每逢周末), I always went to play with my friends and I think it was a good way to keep fit and stay in shape(保持身材) as well. What’s more, when I learnt that my cousin chose volleyball as her PE selection in the Senior High School Entrance Exam next year, I decided to teach her how to play it.

For a start, I’m going to teach her some basic knowledge of playing volleyball, such as how to move her hands, how to shoot and so on. When she’s acquired this knowledge(学会......知识), I’d like to show her some useful skills, such as how to defend, which is particularly helpful and practical in matches. After that, I’ll practice with her every day to make sure she improves quickly and gets a good grade in her test.
answers.summary:
我两年前在我们城市的体育馆里观看了一次现场的排球比赛。整场比赛气氛非常好，选手们在比赛前会拥抱，在比赛中也会给对方加油、打气，排球在他们的传击下舞动、飞跃，非常具有观赏性。
Atmosphere – 氛围
Magnificent – 壮观的
Pique – 激起（兴趣）
Eager – 渴望的
Selection – 选择
Acquire – 掌握
Defend – 防守
Practical – 实用的
Improve – 提高

topics.title: 童年喜欢的玩具/买到开心的东西/礼貌的人

questions.content: Describe a toy you liked in your childhood Describe a thing that you bought and felt pleased about Describe a polite person you know

source_payload.title: 童年喜欢的玩具/买到开心的东西/礼貌的人

sub_questions.content:
Describe a toy you liked in your childhood
Describe a thing that you bought and felt pleased about
Describe a polite person you know

answers.title: 小时候买乐高
answers.content:
I’ve been such a super fan of Harry Potter since an early age that I was always eager for Harry Potter toys or related products. Lego became popular when I was in primary school, which allows you to play with and form certain characters or models from small bricks (碎片).

So I asked my mom to buy me a Lego model that was shaped like the train in Harry Potter, as it would make my bedroom look like a magical(有魔力的) world. However, I was refused because my mom said I would spend too much time playing it. Therefore, I decided to save the money by myself and I worked out that I would be able to afford it in just a few weeks.

During that period, I used to go to the store to see Lego after school every day. The salesgirl impressed me with(以...留下深刻印象) her politeness and warmth, and asked me what I was interested in. She then offered a specific promotion. What's more, she also helped me choose the one that suited me best. Although I didn’t have enough money, she answered all my questions with a big smile and great patience. I think she’s the politest salesperson I’ve met ever.

Finally, I bought the toy three weeks later, and the moment I held it, I almost went wild with happiness (欣喜若狂) because it was just so special to me as a super-lover of Harry Potter. Sadly, just as my mom said, I spent a lot of time playing with it, but it’s worth mentioning that (值得一提的是) the process of saving for it made me more patient.
answers.summary: 我小时候，乐高特别的流行，我想让我妈妈给我买一个哈利波特火车模型为我的房间增添色彩，但她拒绝了，因此我就开始省吃俭用最终在乐高乐园买下了我心仪的火车模型，因为哈利波特火车是作为哈迷的我最喜欢的周边之一，所以能拥有一个我无比开心。虽然最后我发现虽然拼装火车模型的过程很需要耐心，但还是很有意思的。

topics.title: 有趣的家乡传统/第一次吃的食物

questions.content: Describe an interesting tradition in your country Describe a time when you ate something for the first time

source_payload.title: 有趣的家乡传统/第一次吃的食物

sub_questions.content:
Describe an interesting tradition in your country
Describe a time when you ate something for the first time

answers.title: 第一次在百家宴吃到啤酒鱼
answers.content:
In China, there’s a famous traditional custom of having a “hundred family feast”(百家宴) on the Lantern Festival. In short, during the “hundred family feast”, each family in town prepares a banquet and invites their neighbours to enjoy the cuisine.

During the winter vacation of my sophomore year, I went to my friend's home in Guangxi, just in time for the “hundred family feast” in his village. This event carries more weight(意义重大) now. People gather to have delicacies, but in a reformed manner different from the traditions. For example, people advocate an individual serving(分餐制) system and used serving chopsticks and spoons(公筷公勺). We also enjoy traditional performances featuring singing and dancing.

My good friend lives in Yangshuo and a famous snack there is beer fish. I had this delicious dish for the first time at the “hundred family feast”. As soon as it was served on the table, I could smell the fresh aroma of beer, so I couldn’t help but(忍不住) taste it. The fish was tasty, without too many bones in it and without a fishy smell at all. This dish always makes my mouth water(流口水) when I think about it.

I like Yangshuo beer fish very much. It’s not only the dish, but also the best witness of friendship between me and my friends. If I have a chance next time, I would definitely have it again.
answers.summary: 大二寒假的时候，我去广西好朋友家玩。如今，百家宴已经多了更多内涵，大家聚在一起吃特色美食，但是更换了形式，倡导分餐制、公筷公勺，我觉得这样有趣的传统值得推广。好朋友家住在阳朔，阳朔比较著名的小吃就是啤酒鱼，在百家宴上我第一次吃到了这道美味。这道菜我每每想起来都会馋的直流口水，我非常喜欢吃阳朔的啤酒鱼，它不仅仅是一道菜，也是我和朋友情谊的最好见证。如果下次有机会，我一定要再去一次，大快朵颐。

topics.title: 发挥想象力/有趣的谈话/给别人建议（保留）

questions.content: Describe a time you needed to use your imagination Describe an interesting conversation Describe a time when you gave advice to others

source_payload.title: 发挥想象力/有趣的谈话/给别人建议（保留）

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time you needed to use your imagination
Describe an interesting conversation
Describe a time when you gave advice to others

answers.title: 建议对人工智能感兴趣的表妹学相关领域
answers.content:
Last week, my cousin, who’s a senior in high school, came to my home to chat with(与……聊天) me. We talked about how artificial intelligence(人工智能) would develop in the future. It's a very interesting topic because we had totally different points of view and therefore we exerted our utmost efforts(尽最大努力) to fight for our opinions.

As a conservative(保守的) person, I insisted that there would be no big change in the future. But my cousin asked me to use my imagination and picture a robot nanny taking care of all my life needs. Since I had watched many science fiction movies(科幻电影), I easily conjured up(描绘) such a picture. I thought the future would be bright and happy if there were robots assisting us with most of the trivial things in life. I had a feeling that artificial intelligence must be the general direction of the future development of science and technology.

Since my cousin loved it so much, I suggested she select a major related to artificial intelligence at university, so that she can keep abreast of(了解事物的最新情况) the latest developments. To this day, I still look back at this inspiring conversation, and I think it’s interesting because both of us brought brand new ideas to the table.
answers.summary: 上周，正在读高三的表妹来我家找我玩，我们聊起了未来人工智能会发展成什么样。这是个非常有趣的话题因为我们俩观点不同，彼此据理力争。保守派的我坚称未来不会有很大改变的，表妹让我运用想象力，想象未来会有机器人保姆为我服务号一切生活所需，由于我观看的的科技电影比较多，我轻易的描绘出了这样的画面。我发现如果未来生活真的有机器人帮助那是很美好的。我有预感人工智能一定是未来科技发展的大方向，表妹既然如此热爱，就应该在大学去读人工智能相关的专业，这样就可以随时了解人工智能的最新动态。

topics.title: 排久久的队/无聊的经历

questions.content: Describe a time you had to wait in line (queue up) for a long time Describe a time when you felt bored

source_payload.title: 排久久的队/无聊的经历

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time you had to wait in line (queue up) for a long time
Describe a time when you felt bored

answers.title: 为了吃网红店排队真的很无聊
answers.content:
Nobody really likes waiting in a long line as we get easily bored, but that’s just wishful thinking in a very densely populated city, where people need to queue up even to get things like milk and eggs. Just like many others in the city, I also needed to wait in a long queue last week. Of course, I wasn’t trying to buy any eggs, but rather, I was queuing up for an internet-famous Cantonese restaurant when my friend Nicole came to Shanghai to visit me.

At dinner time, we decided to have a feast in this restaurant out of admiration for it, only to find its queue management system had a glitch due to a network-related problem. As we weren’t able to receive its queue information via phone, we had to wait for the staff to call out the numbers as a result. We were required not to go too far away, because if we missed the call, we had to take a new number and start all over again.

I felt extremely bored because I had to spend almost three hours standing in the same area. Trust me, it was way more painful than climbing a mountain, because when you do that, at least you’re walking freely and appreciating the scenery along the way. In contrast, you can’t do anything while waiting in line. However, on the flip side, the Cantonese dim sum tasted great, so it was just about worth it in the end.
answers.summary: 上周朋友Nicole来上海找我玩，我们为了粤菜网红餐厅整整排了3小时的队,我觉得这简直太无聊了。由于店家的网上叫号提醒除了问题，我们只能坐在餐厅门口的凳子上，等待人工叫好。这3小时，我们只能呆在同一个区域，无法自由活动实在是无聊之至。还好最终的粤式点心还算好吃，对我来说有所宽慰。

topics.title: 相见的名人/奇装异服的人（保留）

questions.content: Describe a famous person you are interested in Describe a person who you think wears unusual clothes

source_payload.title: 相见的名人/奇装异服的人（保留）

sub_questions.content:
Describe a famous person you are interested in
Describe a person who you think wears unusual clothes

answers.title: 对衣着特别的GAGA感兴趣
answers.content:
I’m going to talk about Lady Gaga, who’s quite interesting because her sense of style is so unique, or you could say weird. But to be fair, she’s an exceedingly smart(超级聪明) and business-minded female artist. From what I know, she’s launched her own beauty lines too and is making a fortune.

But she’s primarily a musician and I think she’s really talented at it. You see, I first heard her from the song Poker Face. I bet you’ve heard this song as well. The melody is so great and her singing brings the song to life, you have to admit. Later, when I paid more attention to her, I realized she was something else! Her ever-changing fashion sense has definitely made her unique(她的事业的亮点) and her exaggerated make-up and wacky costumes are always underlined. Apparently, her weird style is the very thing that got her so much exposure in the media. From what I’ve read about her, in the early days, she stood out from the streams of cutesy pop princesses (从众多优秀的明星中脱颖而出) with her tiny bodysuits, crazy wigs, sunglasses and her penchant for platform heels. Beetle wings, halos, pyrotechnic bras: you'll find this singer wearing it all. Even though she’s taken her wild style down a notch in recent years,(最近这些年她的狂野风格有所收敛) I still get slightly giddy over her dramatic fashion taste.

So yeah, Lady Gaga made herself a legend by dressing as a legend first. I admire her courage and I’m very interested in where she got these strange fashion ideas from.
answers.summary: 我对穿着风格奇特的LADY GAGA非常感兴趣，我是通过她的作品《poker face》才了解到她的。她出名之前就是一个才华横溢的音乐人。我之所以对她感兴趣是因为她独特的穿衣风格，她都是穿着很高的高跟鞋，夸张的服饰和浓重的妆容，她的衣服材质各式各样，款式也十分新奇，是普通人在日常生活中绝对无法驾驭的。

topics.title: 完美的工作/在国外的短期工作

questions.content: Describe a perfect job you would like to have in the future Describe a short term job you want to have in a foreign country

source_payload.title: 完美的工作/在国外的短期工作

sub_questions.content:
Describe a perfect job you would like to have in the future
Describe a short term job you want to have in a foreign country

answers.title: 试睡员的工作
answers.content:
The perfect job I would like to do in the future／(A short term job ) I want to have in a foreign country is to be a hotel test sleeper. The name might sound strange but I accidentally saw it on the Internet.

Hotel test sleepers are also called hotel critics, and their job responsibility is the same as their job name. They are invited by various luxury hotels to go to that city and then stay in these high-end hotels(高端酒店) for three to five days. After testing the facilities试睡), they need to write down their true evaluation of the hotel. Hotel test sleeping is not just about sleeping in the hotel, but also about experiencing the hotel’s services, environment, hygiene, price, catering(餐饮) and other aspects, such as whether the mattress is soft or hard, the air-conditioning, the speed of the Internet, whether the sewer is blocked, and so on. After the evaluation, a report is written based on the objective experience, which can be used by the hotel to improve the facilities or for reference by guests.

Among all hotels, I want to test the Burj Al Arab(迪拜帆船酒店) in Dubai the most, because that hotel is the most luxurious hotel in the world and all the rooms are very unique. I saw it online and the shape of the hotel resembles(像) a sail. Among all 202 suites, the lowest price is 900 dollars per night, and the 25th floor Royal Suite costs 18, 000 dollars per night. In fact, this is the lowest price in the off-season(淡季), and it’s often impossible to book a room at this price. I can't ever imagine staying even one night there! I really want to experience the difference in a seven-star hotel tour, especially the undersea restaurant. If I could be a test sleeper, I could stay there for free!

(For “a perfect job you would like to do”) For me, this job is simply fantastic. As a hotel test sleeper, you can travel to various cities for free, and you can stay in the best hotels for free. I think it sounds like the perfect job!

(For “A short term job you want to have in a foreign country”) So for me, this is the short term job that would be amazing to have in a foreign country. Because first I would like to be a hotel test sleeper as my future job, and also I would like to explore the only seven-star hotel in the world!
answers.summary: 我将来想做酒店试睡员。我是有一次无意间在网上看到的。那篇文章的内容是盘点新出现的奇奇怪怪的工作，其中一个就是酒店试睡员。他们的工作听起来简直太棒了。他们受到各种奢侈酒店的邀请，首先去到那个城市，然后在高级酒店里面住上三到五天。试睡结束后，他们需要在网上写下对这个酒店的真实评价。我最想去迪拜帆船酒店做试睡员，因为太想体验七星级的酒店之旅有什么不同。这个工作简直不要太好，可以免费去各个城市旅游，还有最好的酒店可以免费住，我觉得听起来简直就是完美！

topics.title: 鼓励别人做不愿做的事/好消息（好消息非本季度考题)

questions.content: Describe a time when you encouraged someone to do something that he/she didn't want to do Describe a time when you received good news

source_payload.title: 鼓励别人做不愿做的事/好消息（好消息非本季度考题)

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time when you encouraged someone to do something that he/she didn't want to do
Describe a time when you received good news

answers.title: 鼓励表弟减肥，得知他减肥成功的消息
answers.content:
I have a cousin who’s in second year (二年级) at college. In fact, he was tested for Hyperglycemia (高血糖) in the middle of the academic year and the whole family was worried. But thankfully, not long ago – maybe a couple of weeks - my aunt called me saying that his condition was much better, and she would like to particularly thank me for that. I was very glad to hear the news because, as a matter of fact, I was the one who talked my cousin into the idea of exercising and losing weight.
He had a bit of anxiety (焦虑) due to a competition at school, and in order to deal/cope with the stress (解压), he only ate food that was high in calories (摄入高能量/卡路里食物), like burgers, chips, cheese, cakes and so on. As a result, it was very clear to see that he’d put on weight. He didn’t intend on ending up like that (本不想变成那样) but he couldn’t seem to control himself (无法控制他自己).
I didn’t want to see him getting into a worse and irreversible situation(在情况糟到无法改变之前), because we’re really good friends and I hoped he could live a healthy and happy life, so I came up with an idea.
I knew that simply turning him off junk food (不让他吃垃圾食品) wouldn’t help much， because he needed some motivation to cope with the pressure. Therefore, I tried encouraging him to do some exercise by buying him a new game called Cyberpunk2077 that everyone was crazy about. Luckily it worked! My cousin started dancing around when I told him that I would reward him with it if he started to do exercise. Although he told me that he wouldn’t be able to exercise every day, he promised to be careful about what he ate. So, I’m really glad now that he’s getting better and proud I played a part.
answers.summary: 表弟目前正读高三，每天作业加班加点，堪比打工人。随其而来的当然是不小的压力。为了解压，表弟每天都会吃很多高能量食品——汉堡薯条，芝士蛋糕，有上次学校体检竟然查出高血糖。由于一起长大，感情很深，我对于他的身体健康状况非常上心，我下定决心督促他减肥。试过不下三次苦口婆心的劝告后，堂弟开始见我就躲。我当然知道他不想通过锻炼减肥，又耗时间又耗体力。但我没有放弃，后来我打听到堂弟很想玩最近很火的赛博朋克。我刚好拿这个作为诱饵——，他愿意节食锻炼，我就愿意给他买。后来自己出国留学，但上星期通过妈妈得知表弟经过三个月

topics.title: 迷路/野生动物

questions.content: Describe a time when you were lost Describe a time when you got close to wild animals

source_payload.title: 迷路/野生动物

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time when you were lost
Describe a time when you got close to wild animals

answers.title: 在森林公园遇到野兔但迷路了
answers.content:
I don’t possess(拥有) a good sense of direction, which has brought me a lot of troubles as well as some unforgettable(难忘的) experiences. Here’s an interesting story that happened just last week. It was a bright sunny day so I went to the forest with my good friend Karl. The forest was so big that we could only find our way around it with the help of a GPS and a map.

We were about to get something to eat when we found some rabbits eating grass a few metres away. We decided to get closer to watch them because both Karl and I are fond of observing(观察) rabbits. As we got closer, we found that they were so alert(警觉) that they quickly ran away. Having been interested by their reaction, we couldn’t help chasing after them. However, man can never run as fast as a rabbit, so we failed to catch up with(追上) them. But what was worse was that we found ourselves lost in the park. Karl was confused about our location, and so was I. I decided to turn to(求助) the GPS for help, but unfortunately, it couldn’t locate us either. I felt bit helpless because I had to rely on(依赖) the map, but together we managed to find our way out. From that moment on, I came to realize the importance of having a good sense of direction.
answers.summary: 上周我和朋友Karl一起去森林公园玩。我们发现几只野兔正在吃草，我们都很喜欢兔子了我们慢慢地靠近它们，但它们受到了惊吓，像远处跑去。我和Karl 不由自主地追寻着野兔的脚步，但它们跑的太快了，我们还是跟丢了。等我们缓过神来却发现自己迷路了。我赶紧拿出入园时拿的公园地图查找位置，终于在我和K的共同努力分辨方向下走回到了大路。我觉得方向感真的太重要了。

topics.title: 聪明的人/有用的书

questions.content: Describe a person who is intelligent Describe a book you read that you find useful

source_payload.title: 聪明的人/有用的书

sub_questions.content:
Describe a person who is intelligent
Describe a book you read that you find useful

answers.title: 聪明的人/有用的书
answers.content:
My grandfather is probably the most intelligent person that I’ve met. He recommended to me a book named The Road Less Travelled by M. Scott Peck. Well, if you also happened to have read this one, you may agree with what I’m about to say.

My grandfather did not even finish his elementary education as his family was destitute(一贫如洗). He entered the workforce at the age of 12 and then taught himself about psychology, and philosophy, as well as commerce. On his bookshelf, there are at least ten worn out dictionaries. It’s all very impressive but even with perseverance (毅力，恒心), I don’t think he could reach the level of a Doctor author like Scott Peck.

My grandfather is exceptionally smart though. He can comprehend new concepts easily. I guess that’s why he could understand the true worth of the book The Road Less Travelled, which is practically an exploration into one’s spiritual and psychological development. He bought the hard cover edition for me as a gift before my college entrance exam, when I was under extreme stress(压力山大) and my sleep pattern was all messed up(睡眠紊乱) due to anxiety and worries about the upcoming exam. This book was so powerful that it lifted my spirit greatly. Unlike many self-help books (励志书) that attempt to offer the readers seemingly helpful tips which often turn out to be worthless, this book helped me find my strength from within. Instead of attacking the negative feelings, I started to accept them and deal with them sensibly. Thanks to this book, I adjusted myself well enough to get good grades in the exam. Till today, I am still thankful to my intelligent grandfather, as well as that great book that he recommended to me.
answers.summary: 我的外公是一个非常聪明的人。因为小时候家境贫穷，其实他连小学都没有毕业就开始进入社会工作了，但是他通过自己的努力去学习，一有时间就看报看书，字典都翻烂了好几本。所以他总是能说出一些非常有哲理的话，也正是因为这样，我觉得他是一个非常聪明的人。我在高考前每天压力都很大，于是外公就经常会给我讲一些鼓舞人心的话语，也会推荐给我一些让人振奋力量的书籍。其中有一本叫《少有人走的路》。

topics.title: 长久的追求 /不喜欢的对话

questions.content: Describe an ambition that you have had for a long time Describe a conversation you were not interested in

source_payload.title: 长久的追求 /不喜欢的对话

sub_questions.content:
Describe an ambition that you have had for a long time
Describe a conversation you were not interested in

answers.title: 长久的追求 /不喜欢的对话
answers.content:
Well, I want to open my own coffee shop. Ideally, it should be located in a quiet alley in the city suburb, which borders a green space(和绿地相邻), or it could even be located right on a green space. That way all my guests will get to enjoy the beauty of nature while they sip their coffee and catch up with their friends or family(和朋友和家人叙旧).

I’ve been cooking this idea up in my head for a very long time. Sometimes I almost get the feeling that this future coffee shop is my destiny. For a start, I’m a heavy coffee drinker myself(我超级爱喝咖啡). Though it sounds like a small dream, I take it seriously. In order to achieve this goal, I’m preparing to go to England to learn about coffee making, as well as some skills about how to run a shop. I hope to bring the European style into my coffee shop. I’ll also go and work in a chain coffee shop for a year or two after I finish my master program in England. You know, I want to practice using the skills I´ll have learned in a real setting.

Unfortunately, my mother believes this is all nonsense. In her mind, I should work as a civil servant. Well, she has a point. In China, a civil servant enjoys high social status and gets paid quite well. We had a big argument when she came to visit me last month. She tried to persuade me to quit the idea of studying overseas. To her, running a cafe is barely a proper job. I was pretty upset with her because of this, but we eventually agreed to disagree(我们同意尊重彼此的意见) and I’ll continue to pursue my dream. Maybe my mother will change her opinion someday.
answers.summary: 我妈妈一直觉得公务员是一个非常稳定的职业。上个月她来上海看我的时候，还在劝我安心考公务员，不要出国折腾了，她很担心我能不能在国外独立生活。

topics.title: 改变想法/坦率的人

questions.content: Describe a time when you changed your opinion Describe a person who you think is very open

source_payload.title: 改变想法/坦率的人

sub_questions.content:
Describe a time when you changed your opinion
Describe a person who you think is very open

answers.title: 改变想法/坦率的人
answers.content:
There’s a popular live show on TV called Rock & Roast at the moment. It’s actually a talk show program. I never thought I’d be interested in it, because most talk show programs suck(好多脱口秀节目都糟透了). The guests are usually dull and have no edge(毫无特色). Also, such shows are often heavily commercialized(被商业化). It’s really a waste of time to watch them.

But a girl on this show changed my mind about talk shows. Her name is Yangli, a quick-minded and straightforward girl. Unlike other guests who always like to big themselves up by using big words, Yangli speaks about the plain truth. She has a sharp tongue, but she doesn’t talk rudely. I resonate with a lot of her opinions on many social issues(她的观点可以引起我的共鸣). Oh, yeah I forgot to mention that she’s a feminist. But as far as I understand, men don’t dislike her. She has tons of male fans on the Internet. That has something to do with her language skills I’m sure. I mean, she backs up women without insulting men. That takes some real effort in structuring one’s language, doesn’t it? Most importantly, Yangli has a great sense of humor. While you are laughing about the funny content that she offers, you always get something to chew on with your friends afterwards.

For an opinionated person(固执的人) like myself to change their mind is no easy task. That’s why I think this girl is brilliant and impressive! She is the most straight forward talk show person I’ve seen!
answers.summary: 最近有一档很火的节目叫《脱口秀大会》。其实我本身是一个对各类综艺节目都不太感冒的人，因为我觉得看综艺节目非常浪费时间。但是在朋友的一再推荐下，我决定看一看这档综艺节目。在看了几期之后，它彻底改变了我对综艺节目的刻板印象。我本来觉得所有的综艺节目都非常没有内涵，但是优秀的脱口秀演员们打破了我的看法，也因此我改变了我的看法。我特别喜欢一个女脱口秀演员，叫杨笠。她是个特别坦率的人，直白也是她的脱口秀最明显的风格。

topics.title: 喜欢的公共建筑/放松的地方/好消息

questions.content: Describe an impressive public building that you enjoyed visiting Describe a place that can be good to relax Describe a time when you received good news

source_payload.title: 喜欢的公共建筑/放松的地方/好消息

sub_questions.content:
Describe an impressive public building that you enjoyed visiting
Describe a place that can be good to relax
Describe a time when you received good news

answers.title: 喜欢的公共建筑/放松的地方/好消息
answers.content:
You know, the place that I find most relaxing must be my college library. The library building was a unique M shape. There are different versions(不同说法) of what the M stands for. Most people believe that it represents ‘more’, ‘cause through reading, we’ll learn more, experience more and become wiser.

Throughout my college years, library reading was an essential part of my life. In fact, I was probably the most frequent library user that I’ve ever met. I visited it almost on a daily basis(我基本每天都去). It’s just the most laid back place(最放松的地方) I could ever find! I’m not sure if you share this feeling, but when I’m surrounded by books, I feel a great sense of security. Plus, the library offered great reference materials that could illuminate my understanding of many topics(启发我对很多事情的理解). And best of all, I could borrow any book I wanted. Nothing felt better than coming back to the dorm with an armful of books(很多书)!

Now that I’ve left college, I visit the libraries in my hometown. And just recently, I heard the good news from my mother that my hometown library will get subsidized by the local government. I logged into the official website of the library in question and saw the planning diagram of the future library. Wow, it’ll be splendid! Apparently, the library will be expanded to a great extent and all the old facilities will be replaced by modern ones. There will even be AI equipment to support the readers locating books. It seems the future’s here already, isn’t that amazing?
answers.summary: 我是一个非常热爱读书的人，所以我最喜欢的公共建筑，毫无疑问就是图书馆了，并且，在图书馆中我都会感到很放松。图书馆对我来说是我在大学期间放松的最好去处。我的大学图书馆非常有特色，因为它的外形是一个 “M”的形状。对于这个“M”的由来，有许多不同的解释，有人说它代表“more”，因为读书让人获得更多的知识。

topics.title: 室内游戏/短期外国工作/遇到朋友时惊讶的情形

questions.content: Describe an experience that you enjoyed an indoor game Describe a job you would like to do in a foreign country for a short term Describe a time when you were surprised to meet a friend

source_payload.title: 室内游戏/短期外国工作/遇到朋友时惊讶的情形

sub_questions.content:
Describe an experience that you enjoyed an indoor game
Describe a job you would like to do in a foreign country for a short term
Describe a time when you were surprised to meet a friend

answers.title: 室内游戏/短期外国工作/惊喜遇到的朋友
answers.content:
There’s an indoor game I like very much, called Escape Room, also called TAKAGISM, which is now popular domestically and internationally(国内和国外). The main ideas for escape room interactive games(互动游戏) are mostly derived from(来自于) scenes from movies and from ideas on the Internet. They are generally more interesting and challenging, and bring exciting experiences. Escape rooms can derive different themes due to(因为) different design ideas, from ancient tombs to wildernesses, from stealing, to escaping prison. The game will trap three to four players in a room and then within a limited time, the players have to help each other find clues, solve puzzles, and escape the room.

The first time I experienced such a room was three years ago, when I was an exchange student in the UK. That day was my 18th birthday, and I wanted to do something different, so my good friends and I went to the newly opened escape room near our school. Coincidentally(巧合地), I was pleasantly surprised to meet my classmate Monica there, and found out that she was working part-time at that business. Following her recommendation, I experienced three different escape rooms, all of which were very fun. They each tested the players’ puzzle solving abilities(解谜能力). I am still impressed with(印象深刻) that experience.

This year I will go to the UK for my postgraduate studies, so I contacted my friend Monica and learned that she still works part-time at that place, and that they are recruiting students for part-time jobs. I really like escape rooms, so I applied for this position. I believe this part-time job will add a lot of fun to my one-year graduate life in the UK.
answers.summary: 我非常喜欢一款室内游戏，叫密室逃脱。密室逃脱是现在风靡海内外的一款室内游戏。游戏会将三到四名玩家困在一个密室中，在限定的时间里，玩家要互相帮助，寻找线索来逃脱密室。我第一次玩这个游戏是在我英国交换的时候。那一天是我的生日，我想这要做一些不一样的事情，于是来到了学校附近新开的密室逃脱游戏店。巧的是，那一天，我惊喜地遇到了我的同班同学莫妮卡，她当时在那家店做兼职。在她的推荐下我体验了三个不同的密室，都非常地好玩，很考验玩家的解谜能力。

topics.title: 忘记的重要的事情/好决定

questions.content: Describe an occasion when you forgot something important Describe a good decision you made recently

source_payload.title: 忘记的重要的事情/好决定

sub_questions.content:
Describe an occasion when you forgot something important
Describe a good decision you made recently

answers.title: 忘记的重要的事情/好决定
answers.content:
I recently made a very important decision, which is to plan everything ahead. Actually, I am not a person who likes to make plans. I always procrastinate(拖延) in doing things. But last week, I forgot a very important thing that made me feel really guilty.
